parsed.summary: Cannot complete a defensible adversarial review of this 6,600+ line, 84-file delta without access to the actual diff content. From the available metadata, two material concerns surface: (1) scripts/lib/process.mjs was deleted entirely — any surviving import will crash at runtime, and the diff stat alone cannot confirm all consumers were updated; (2) v0.4.6 ships BigModel error dispatch and retry/backoff with unit tests but no UAT evidence (v0.4.4 and v0.4.5 have UAT reports, v0.4.6 does not). These are enough to block until verified.

findings.title: scripts/lib/process.mjs deleted entirely — dangling-import crash risk unverified
findings.body: The diff stat shows scripts/lib/process.mjs went from 67 lines to 0 (full deletion). Concurrent modifications to render.mjs (79 lines changed) and glm-companion.mjs (139 lines changed) may have removed direct imports, but without seeing the actual diff content it is impossible to confirm that every consumer — including scripts/check-imports.mjs (also modified, 5 lines changed), session-lifecycle-hook.mjs, job-control.mjs, tracked-jobs.mjs, or any dynamic import() calls — was updated. A single surviving `import ... from './process.mjs'` or `import ... from './lib/process.mjs'` will produce an uncatchable MODULE_NOT_FOUND at require time, crashing the entire CLI entry point. The check-imports.mjs script was also modified, which could mean it was updated to exclude process.mjs from validation — hiding rather than fixing the problem. This risk is amplified by the fact that tests mock module boundaries and would not surface a missing-file import in production entry-point resolution.
findings.recommendation: Run `grep -r 'process\.mjs' scripts/` against the working tree to confirm zero surviving references. If check-imports.mjs was modified to exclude process.mjs from its validation set, revert that exclusion and fix the root cause instead. Add an integration test that imports every entry point without mocking to catch unresolved modules.

findings.title: No UAT evidence for v0.4.6 — BigModel error dispatch and retry/backoff untested at integration level
findings.body: Commit e0a9ba8 introduces BigModel error dispatch, auto retry/backoff, and retires verify-release.yml. Unit tests exist (tests/retry.test.mjs at 352 lines, tests/bigmodel-errors.test.mjs at 189 lines) but the test-automation/uat-reports/ directory contains reports only for v0.4.4 and v0.4.5. There is no v0.4.6 UAT report directory, no run-uat.sh, and no scenario JSON files. The retry and error-dispatch paths are exactly the kind of code that passes unit tests but fails under real API latency, rate-limiting, socket timeouts, and partial response bodies. Shipping retry logic without end-to-end validation means the first real-world failure mode will be discovered in production.
findings.recommendation: Create test-automation/uat-reports/v0.4.6/ with scenario files covering: (a) transient 429 from BigModel with retry exhaustion, (b) unrecognized error code falling through dispatch, (c) retry after partial JSON response, (d) backoff timing under concurrent requests. Do not ship retry logic without at least one UAT run against a live or recorded API.

findings.title: Scope of change exceeds safe review surface — 84 files, 6,600+ lines without structured review tooling
findings.body: The diff from v0.4.0 to HEAD spans 84 files and 6,623 insertions / 480 deletions. This includes new modules (retry.mjs at 203 lines, bigmodel-errors.mjs at 168 lines), modified state management (state.mjs, tracked-jobs.mjs, job-control.mjs), and a rewritten client (glm-client.mjs at 155 lines changed). Even with the CI quality gate and pr-check workflows added, there is no evidence that this cumulative delta was reviewed as a coherent change. Individual commit-level review can miss cross-cutting invariants (e.g., retry.mjs interacting with state.mjs lock semantics, or bigmodel-errors.mjs classification mismatches with glm-client.mjs error shapes). The user focus on 'v0.4.7 sampling-sweep run 51 (C3-v04x-cumulative)' implies this is a sweep review, but without access to the actual diff hunks, adversarial analysis is limited to structural inference.
findings.recommendation: Require a full inter-file dependency trace for the new retry.mjs → bigmodel-errors.mjs → glm-client.mjs → state.mjs chain. Verify that retry state is isolated per-request and cannot leak across concurrent job submissions. Run the CI quality gate against the full cumulative diff, not just the latest commit.

next_steps: Provide the actual diff content (git diff v0.4.0..HEAD) so a proper line-level adversarial review can be performed — the current finding set is constrained by lack of code visibility.
Run `grep -rn 'process\.mjs\|process\(\' scripts/ commands/` to confirm the deleted module has no surviving references anywhere in the runtime path.
Execute at least one UAT run for v0.4.6 retry/backoff behavior against a live or recorded BigModel endpoint before tagging any v0.4.7 release.
Verify check-imports.mjs still validates imports against the current file set and was not modified to silently skip the deleted process.mjs.
